concerns.text: No task implements **FR‑001** – the system must accept a list of URLs as input.
No task implements **FR‑002** – automatic extraction of sample size, effect size, and p‑value into `ABSummary` entities.
No task implements **FR‑003** – reconstruction of p‑values using the appropriate statistical test.
No task implements **FR‑004** – flagging a summary as inconsistent when the absolute p‑value or effect‑size difference exceeds the 0.05/5 % thresholds.
No task implements **FR‑025** – a priori power‑analysis guaranteeing N ≥ 300 (or the calculated minimum) for the binomial prevalence test.
No task implements **FR‑031** – evaluation of the inconsistency‑detection component on the synthetic validation dataset (precision ≥ 90 %, recall ≥ 80 %).
No task implements **FR‑032** – subgroup prevalence analysis per domain/year with Fisher’s exact test for groups ≥ 10 summaries.
No task addresses **SC‑001** – verification that extraction accuracy is ≥ 95 % on a manually annotated validation set.
No task addresses **SC‑003** – Monte‑Carlo vs. library result difference ≤ 0.01 for each statistical test.
No task addresses **SC‑005** – aggregation and check that parsing‑error rate stays ≤ 5 % of total summaries.
No task addresses **SC‑013** – CI pipeline must exit with status 0 and produce a `manifest.json` in ≥ 99 % of runs.
No task addresses **SC‑014** – binomial test must report p‑value (3 dp), Wilson CI width ≤ 0.10, and flag significance at α = 0.05.
No task addresses **SC‑015** – sensitivity analysis must show prevalence variation < 0.02 across baseline 0.02–0.10.
No task addresses **SC‑024** – exported `summary_report.csv` must contain the exact columns and values matching those in `audit_report.json`.
No task addresses **SC‑025** – enforce that the audited corpus size N ≥ 300 (or the power‑analysis minimum).
No task addresses **SC‑026** – Monte‑Carlo validation of all statistical test implementations with absolute difference ≤ 0.01.
No task addresses **SC‑027** – generate a bias‑assessment report confirming no domain exceeds 30 % and include both raw and bias‑adjusted rates in final outputs.
No task addresses **SC‑028** – user‑test or timing benchmark to verify the Quickstart guide can be completed on 30 URLs within 30 minutes.
No task addresses **SC‑030** – synthetic‑validation precision/recall/F1 thresholds (≥ 90 % precision, ≥ 80 % recall).
No task addresses **SC‑032** – for each subgroup (domain or year) with ≥ 10 summaries, compute Fisher’s exact test and report subgroup prevalence and p‑value.
Principle VII (Source Provenance & Transparency) is not covered by any task – URLs and source identifiers must be recorded alongside extracted metrics.
Principle I (Reproducibility) – random‑seed pinning for Monte‑Carlo validation and synthetic data generation is absent from the task list.
Principle VI (Statistical Consistency Verification) – no dedicated task to ensure that any > 0.05 discrepancy is documented in an audit notebook.
Task T013 mentions manifest generation but lacks a follow‑up validation step to verify content‑hashes against the Constitution’s Versioning Discipline (Principle V).
